## Issue to Resolve

Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Autoformatting should not reformat below line to incorrect state
```
private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Observed Behavior
Autoformatting moves `DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))` to the next line and as the results it looks like this:
```
    private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = 
    DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Autoformatting should allow to reproduce this. My Detekt config:
```
formatting:
    excludes: [ '**/build/**' ]
    MaximumLineLength:
        active: false
    NoBlankLineBeforeRbrace:
        active: false
    Indentation:
        active: false
    ArgumentListWrapping:
        active: false
    MultiLineIfElse:
        active: false
```

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.1
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used: Detekt 1.23.0. Related issue https://github.com/detekt/detekt/issues/6223
* Version of Gradle: 7.3.3
* Kotlin version 1.8.21

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyWrappingRule.kt`

 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }
171→}

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. Let me trace through the logic:

The `requireNewlineAfterLeaf` function (used when wrapping after `=`) calls `indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix)` which calls `node.indent().plus(indent)`. The `node.indent()` gets the indent of the `=` sign's node, then adds one more level of indentation.

But the `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` function (used when wrapping before `CALL_EXPRESSION`) calls `node.indent()` which gets the indent of the **call expression** node itself. Since the call expression is a child of the PROPERTY node, `node.indent()` would return the indent of the PROPERTY's line (the line containing `val`), without adding an extra indent level.

So when the property value (CALL_EXPRESSION) is wrapped to the next line because it exceeds max line length, `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` inserts a newline with the same indentation as the property declaration itself, instead of indenting it one level deeper.

Let me also look at what happens when `=` overflows - in that case `requireNewlineAfterLeaf` is called on the `=` node and `childIndentOf` properly adds one indent level. But when `=` fits but the CALL_EXPRESSION doesn't, `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` is called on the call expression, and `node.indent()` just returns the current indent (the property's indent) without adding a child level.

The fix should be in `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` when called for the `CALL_EXPRESSION` case. The call expression should be indented as a child of the property, not at the same level.

Let me look more carefully at how `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` is used:

content.text:
Now I see the issue clearly:

- `requireNewlineAfterLeaf` (used for wrapping after `:` and `=`) uses `indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix)` which is `node.indent() + indent` — this correctly adds a child indent level.
- `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` (used for wrapping before `TYPE_REFERENCE` and `CALL_EXPRESSION`) uses `node.indent()` — this just gets the current indent of the node, which is the property's indent level, NOT the child indent level.

For TYPE_REFERENCE and CALL_EXPRESSION, when they're wrapped to a new line, they should be indented as children, i.e., one level deeper than the property declaration. The fix is to use `indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)` instead of `node.indent()` in `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf`.

Let me verify by looking at how the TYPE_REFERENCE test case works - in the test, after wrapping before `TypeWithALongName`, the result shows it indented with 4 spaces (one indent level deeper). Let me check if `node.indent()` would already return the correct value in that case... 

Actually, `node.indent()` returns the indent of the **current** node (looks backward for the most recent newline and returns the whitespace after it). For a `TYPE_REFERENCE` or `CALL_EXPRESSION` that's a child of a `PROPERTY`, `indent()` returns the indent of the PROPERTY (the line the node is on), which would be the same level. But the test expects it to be one level deeper...

Wait, let me re-read the test case for TYPE_REFERENCE wrapping:
```
val aVariableWithALongerName:
    TypeWithALongName
```

This is indented with 4 spaces. And the property `val` is at column 0 (0 indent). So the TYPE_REFERENCE after wrapping should be at indent level 1 (4 spaces). Since `node.indent()` for the TYPE_REFERENCE would return `""` (since it's on the same line as `val`, the indent of that line is nothing/empty), and `indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)` would return `"\n" + "" + "    "` = `"\n    "`.

Hmm wait, let me re-check. `indent()` includes a newline by default. Let me look again:

```kotlin
public fun ASTNode.indent(includeNewline: Boolean = true): String =
    leaves(forward = false)
        .firstOrNull { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
        ?.text
        ?.substringAfterLast('\n')
        .orEmpty()
        .applyIf(includeNewline) { prefixIfNot("\n") }
```

So `node.indent()` returns `"\n"` + (whitespace before the line). For a top-level property, that would be `"\n"`. For a 4-space indented property, it would be `"\n    "`.

`indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)` returns `node.indent().plus(indent)` = `"\n" + "" + "    "` = `"\n    "` for a top-level property.

So `node.indent()` = `"\n"` and `childIndentOf(node)` = `"\n    "`.

For the `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf`, the current code uses `node.indent()` which inserts `"\n"` — that's the SAME indent level as the property, not one level deeper. But the tests say it should be `"\n    "` (one level deeper). How do the existing tests pass?

Let me look at `upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe` more carefully. The call is `node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())`. The `node` here is the TYPE_REFERENCE or CALL_EXPRESSION.

Wait, `node.indent()` finds the indent by looking backward from the node for the nearest whitespace-with-newline. For a node like TYPE_REFERENCE that is inline on the same line as `val aVariableWithALongerName: `, the `indent()` would find the newline before `val` and return the indent before `val`. So for a top-level property, `node.indent()` = `"\n"`.

But the tests show that with `addAdditionalRuleProvider { IndentationRule() }`, the result has proper indentation. Maybe the IndentationRule fixes the indent afterward. Let me check if the tests are really relying on PropertyWrappingRule alone or if IndentationRule is doing the heavy lifting on the indent.

Let me look at the test more carefully:

```kotlin
propertyWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
    .setMaxLineLength()
    .addAdditionalRuleProvider { IndentationRule() }
    .hasLintViolation(2, 30, "Missing newline before \"TypeWithALongName\"")
    .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
```

So the IndentationRule is added as an additional rule. The PropertyWrappingRule inserts the newline (possibly with wrong indent), and then IndentationRule fixes the indent. That explains why existing tests pass.

But the issue is that when the IndentationRule is NOT active (as in the user's config where `Indentation: active: false`), the PropertyWrappingRule inserts the newline with incorrect indentation.

Now the question: should `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` use `indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)` instead of `node.indent()`?

Looking at the two call sites:
1. Wrapping before TYPE_REFERENCE (line 106): The type should be indented as a child
2. Wrapping before CALL_EXPRESSION (line 123): The call expression should be indented as a child

Both should use child indent. The fix is to change `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` to use `indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)`.

Let me also verify: in `requireNewlineAfterLeaf`, `indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix)` is called where `nodeToFix` defaults to `nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired` (which is `=` or `:`). `childIndentOf` calls `node.indent()` on the `=`/`:` node and then adds one indent level. The `=`/`:` node's indent is the same as the property's indent, so this correctly produces property indent + one level.

For `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf`, `node` is the TYPE_REFERENCE or CALL_EXPRESSION, whose indent is also the property's indent. So `indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)` would give property indent + one level — correct!

Let me make the fix:

 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),

 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }

 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }

 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node

 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return

130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()

134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }

137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,

143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }

153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(

162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }

content.text:
The fix is clean and minimal. Let me summarize what was done:

## Root Cause

In `PropertyWrappingRule.kt`, the `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` method (used when wrapping before `TYPE_REFERENCE` and `CALL_EXPRESSION` nodes) was using `node.indent()` to determine the indentation for the new line. The `indent()` function returns the indentation of the current line (i.e., the property declaration's indent level), which means the wrapped value would be placed at the **same** indent level as the property itself.

For example, with a 4-space indented property:
```kotlin
    private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = 
    DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```
The value is at the same indent as the `private val` — **incorrect**.

## Fix

Changed line 149 from:
```kotlin
node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
```
to:
```kotlin
node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
```

`indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)` returns `node.indent() + indent` (one additional indent level), which is consistent with `requireNewlineAfterLeaf` (used for wrapping after `:` and `=`). This ensures the wrapped value is properly indented one level deeper than the property declaration:

```kotlin
    private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription =
        DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

All existing tests pass with this change.

 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }
171→}
